title: Custom Hook Finale
description:
The finale. This stopwatch works, but all of its logic lives inside the component. Your job is to extract it into a reusable custom hook.

Create a custom hook named `useSeconds` (a function whose name starts with `use`) that owns the state and the interval effect and returns the current seconds. Then call it from `Stopwatch`: `const seconds = useSeconds();`.
objectives: Define `function useSeconds() { ... }` holding the state + effect, returning `seconds`
In Stopwatch, get the value via `const seconds = useSeconds();`
hints: A custom hook is just a function whose name starts with `use` that calls other hooks.
Move `useState` and the `useEffect` interval into `useSeconds`, and `return seconds` from it.
The component becomes tiny: `const seconds = useSeconds();` then render `{seconds}`.

checks.description: Defines a custom hook named useSeconds

checks.failMessage: Call `const seconds = useSeconds();` in the component.
